title: Washington's resentment toward 'paper tiger' NATO is growing
subhed: The Trump administration has accused NATO allies of failing to show solidarity with the United States during the war with Iran. Its frustration is particularly strong toward Spain, France and the United Kingdom.

title: Driven by women and solo travelers, small group tours attract renewed interest
subhed: Despite the war in the Middle East, small group travel has seen a resurgence in recent years. Altaï, one of the major French players in the sector, announced it would be acquired by Intrepid Travel, the Australian company that is one of the global leaders in the market.

title: 'Many see Trump's plans to reshape Washington as a metaphor for his governing style'
subhed: In an interview, Lawrence Vale, professor of urban planning at MIT, explains how the American president is striving to leave his mark on the federal capital through initiatives such as the Triumphal Arch, White House ballroom and statues.

title: 'How can anyone love Céline Dion? The hysteria surrounding her Paris concerts is astonishing'
subhed: After the spectacular sale of nearly 500,000 tickets for a series of shows in Paris this autumn, Le Monde columnist Michel Guerrin questions in his column what drives people to love (or hate) the singer.

title: Sealing the internet: How Putin wants to cut Russia off from the rest of the world
subhed: Controlling information has become a matter of survival for the Russian leader. Since 2019, he has sought to create a 'sovereign RuNet' under his control and cut off from the world. The technical implementation of this project has accelerated since the start of the war in Ukraine in 2022.
